problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files with walkFiles() fallback, excludes only the dispatch artifact root, and has test coverage for both git-backed and non-git project mutation detection. Dirty working tree confirmed with 906 git-tracked/cached/other files.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() function uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard to get tracked, cached, and other files, returning null on failure to trigger walkFiles() fallback.
evidence.why: Primary evidence that git-tracked/cached/other files are covered by boundary snapshot via git ls-files command.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() function provides non-git filesystem fallback using readdirSync, excludes .git/node_modules/dist/coverage/.next/.cache directories via excludedDir(), and returns repo-relative file paths.
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git projects are supported via filesystem walk when git ls-files fails.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() || walkFiles() for dual-mode file discovery, computes SHA256 digests via fileDigest(), and excludes allowed write roots (artifact directories) from the snapshot map.
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot logic combining git ls-files and filesystem walk, excluding only allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after SHA256 maps to detect added, modified, or deleted files, returning sorted changes with path and change type.
evidence.why: Boundary violation detection algorithm that identifies mutations detected by snapshots.

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot captured before agent execution loop begins with allowed write roots exclusion.
evidence.why: Evidence that baseline filesystem state is established before any agent execution.

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter snapshot captured after all agents complete, diff performed via diffBoundarySnapshots(), and result.boundary populated with project_mutation status and changes array.
evidence.why: Evidence that post-execution boundary audit detects any filesystem mutations.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with path and change details, and prd_ready forced to false when blockers exist.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations directly block PRD readiness as required by acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations initializes git repo, spawns agent that writes untracked-mutation.txt, asserts status=blocked, project_mutation=violated, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker present.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming boundary audit detects untracked file additions in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: Test non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts creates pre-existing src/existing.txt, spawns agent that modifies project file and writes to runtimeDir, asserts violation detected for src/existing.txt but runtimeDir artifact excluded.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming non-git fallback works and correctly excludes allowed artifact writes from boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Working tree shows 45 modified files and 9 untracked files including src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts in untracked state.
evidence.why: Evidence confirming the demand status_quo claim about dirty working tree with untracked evidence-dispatch implementation files.

evidence.summary: Git reports 906 tracked/cached/other files in the working tree, confirming git ls-files coverage for boundary snapshot.
evidence.why: Evidence that the git ls-files command successfully captures tracked/cached/other files as required.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() function filters out .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, and .cache directories from filesystem walks.
evidence.why: Evidence that standard dependency and build artifacts are excluded from boundary snapshots while still monitoring project source files.

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact shows boundary.project_mutation=clean, allowed_write_roots=[.yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-20260601031036907-onhmy7], and changes=[], confirming artifact root exclusion worked.
evidence.why: Empirical evidence from real dispatch run that boundary audit excluded artifact directory and detected no mutations.

risks: If git ls-files -co --exclude-standard fails silently without triggering walkFiles() fallback, boundary snapshot could miss files in non-git repos
If agents create files in excluded directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) then symlink them to project source, boundary checks could be bypassed
If buildBoundarySnapshot has filesystem race conditions during agent execution, mutations between snapshots could be missed
missing: Stress test results with real Claude -p agents confirming boundary detection in production conditions (status_quo mentions need for stress testing)

result.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: (1) git ls-files -co --exclude-standard covers tracked/cached/other files at lines 62-69, (2) walkFiles() fallback at lines 71-85 handles non-git projects, (3) buildBoundarySnapshot() excludes only allowed write roots at lines 97-108, (4) tests confirm git-backed detection at lines 416-452 and non-git fallback at lines 454-494, (5) dirty working tree confirmed with 906 git-tracked files and untracked evidence-dispatch files. Only missing item is stress test with real Claude -p agents as acknowledged in status_quo.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness: all project claims are backed by code, tests, logs, or artifacts; no assumptions presented as facts; the single missing item (stress test with real Claude -p agents) is correctly acknowledged in both the demand status_quo and explorer missing array.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard to capture tracked/cached/other files, returning null on failure to trigger fallback.
evidence.why: Verifies git ls-files coverage claim with exact code location and implementation details.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides non-git fallback using readdirSync, excludes standard directories, returns repo-relative paths.
evidence.why: Verifies fallback walk implementation claim with code evidence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines gitFiles() || walkFiles() and excludes allowed write roots from snapshot.
evidence.why: Verifies dual-mode file discovery and artifact exclusion claim.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations with git repo initialization and untracked file write.
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage claim for git-backed mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Test non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files while ignoring artifacts in runtimeDir.
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage claim for non-git fallback and artifact exclusion.

evidence.summary: 45 modified files, 9 untracked files including evidence-dispatch implementation files in untracked state.
evidence.why: Verifies dirty working tree claim with actual git status output.

evidence.summary: Git reports 906 tracked/cached/other files in working tree.
evidence.why: Verifies the 906 files claim used in explorer summary.

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact shows boundary.project_mutation=clean with allowed_write_roots exclusion and empty changes array.
evidence.why: Verifies empirical evidence from real dispatch run that artifact exclusion works correctly.

risks: If explorer incorrectly classified any external research as project scope, verification would miss the source category mismatch
If any evidence paths are relative to wrong root, the verification would pass but links would be unusable

result.notes: All evidence verified: (1) every evidence record has proper repo-relative path or locator, (2) all scopes are correctly classified as project for code/tests/logs/artifacts, (3) all sources match the evidence type (project_code, project_test, project_log, project_artifact), (4) no assumptions presented as facts - assumptions array is empty, (5) the single missing item (stress test with real Claude -p agents) is correctly reflected in missing array and was explicitly acknowledged in demand status_quo, (6) risks are appropriately identified as technical concerns rather than blockers. Evidence quality is high with exact line numbers and implementation details. Ready to proceed to cross_check phase.
